summary.rationale: Strong employee sentiment; moderate innovation; 40 reviews

reviews.title: Jensen’s Kingdom
reviews.pros:
CEO has vision
Pay is high
Stock growth 
Ambitious vision
First principles
reviews.cons:
Can be chaotic 
High pressure 
Large corporation

reviews.title: Thank you NVIDIA and CEO Huang
reviews.pros: The opportunities to forge a career path that aligned to my interests and talents along with the supportive culture.  The benefits afforded to employees are second to none.  Being a part of this organization since the launch of DGX in 2016 has been very fulfilling and incredibility rewarding.
reviews.cons: Sales overall at NVIDIA are often treated as second class citizens instead of professional assets - this is especially the case, unfortunately, in our Healthcare organization.  Too often we are tasked with to do's while not being any part of the discussions on what to do or why.
reviews.advice: At some point customers will not knock on our door as often as they do today.  Upskilling our technical team members on how to ask questions, versus dispensing advice, the importance of be inquisitive instead of teachers, could better prepare for that day.

reviews.title: Think twice if you’re a parent
reviews.pros: Great leadership, product, vision. Kind, hard working people.
reviews.cons:
Horrible place to work for parents. Apply elsewhere if you want to spend the holidays with your kids. Plan on working nights & weekends regularly (all the time when you don’t have childcare). There’s no onsite gym or any daycare options…definitely missing the benefits of a big tech company. Quarterly “free days” are almost never free—just frustrating if you’ve planned something fun, that you can no longer do.  

Very volatile and hard to predict when your weekend will blow up…which leads to constant anxiety.
reviews.advice: If you’d like to attract and maintain a diverse workforce that includes working parents, consider adding benefits that make it easier, not harder to thrive at your company. If you don’t care about attracting parents, then I’d continue doing what you’re doing.

reviews.title: Fast-Paced, Team-Dependent
reviews.pros: Competitive pay/stock and solid benefits, strong company name, newer office spaces depending on location, and decent opportunities for internal transfers and career mobility.
reviews.cons: Experience is very team-dependent, expectations are high with limited onboarding/guidance, communication and processes can feel unclear, and parking/office space limitations can be frustrating.

reviews.title: CEO is a rock star, company encourages collaboration
reviews.pros: It is a rare company that encourages collaboration across teams. The first principle thinking is encouraged from the top.
reviews.cons: Slow climb on career ladder. If you are seeking to cimb up the ladder fast, NVIDIA might not be the ideal place.

reviews.title: Good company overall, but a lot depends on the team. Great work/life balance. Compensation and bureaucracy issues.
reviews.pros: You can meet great collegues and if you are lucky with management you can have a freedom to work on the topics you consider interesting and important with little pressure and change of direction every few months. Great work/life balance. And of course you can publish your work even in ML/robotics areas.
reviews.cons: Depending on the team, but bureaucracy can be quite a limiting factor. Compensation is on the lower spectrum compared to other top tier companies. If not a rate of stock price growth it would be a disaster.

reviews.title: Great Company
reviews.pros: WLB is good, smart people
reviews.cons: Nothing that comes to mind

reviews.title: Great place to work - Changing Rapidly - Very Demanding
reviews.pros: Cutting-edge work, great pay, great team members
reviews.cons:
Cutting-edge work - means you have to keep up with the cutting-edge.  This takes time and energetic curiosity.
Great team members - they demand you be great also - this takes time and can be challenging
reviews.advice: No advice, just a comment.  Great culture at NV, obviously culture is very important and with tremendous growth there is no way not to have a culture change. It's a conundrum.  Personally, I wouldn't work at any other company.

reviews.title: Good place to work
reviews.pros: Good place to work . Employee friendly
reviews.cons: None that I can think

reviews.title: Best place to work!
reviews.pros: Nvidia cares its employees, takes great care of their families. Great supportive and transparent culture. I learned a lot from the company's quarterly meetings by Jensen.
reviews.cons: Large size company. Can have multiple teams working on the same project,.

reviews.title: rewarding but demanding

reviews.title: Good fast paced work environmen
reviews.pros: Expectations are high but it is team dependent

reviews.title: Good
reviews.pros: Fast paced, learn a lot
reviews.cons: Tough environment and competitive internally

reviews.title: Amazing experience at Nvidia
reviews.pros: Opportunity to work with some of the brightest people in the industry
reviews.cons: Since I have joined, I haven't experienced any major negativity in the company culture.

reviews.title: Wonderful Internship Experience
reviews.pros:
Depends on team but I had lots of room for exploration 
Ability to make connections beyond just your team/industry 
Great intern cohort, lot's of ways to make friends with people your age
reviews.cons: Housing is expensive in Santa Clara

reviews.title: Speed of light
reviews.pros: Devs moving fast, engs are very engaged in every aspect.
reviews.cons: WLB is not that good.

reviews.title: Nvidia
reviews.pros: Pay, smart colleagues, interesting work
reviews.cons: Stressful middle management, not a lot of visibility

reviews.title: Salary
reviews.pros: Pay and benefit is good
reviews.cons: Work culture may not be as good

reviews.title: Best place to work
reviews.pros: Great culture, leadership, and growth opportunities
reviews.cons: Nothing to mention, best place to work

reviews.title: Great company, a bit slow process
reviews.pros:
Very professional interviewers
Great work-life balance
Great research reputation
reviews.cons: Recurriting process is really slow and full of chaos

reviews.title: Great place to work!  Cutting edge
reviews.pros:
* Pay!!!  (OMG)
* Not as big as it seems - NVIDIA is a composite of many small groups, so it has the advantages of being a big company without the disadvantages of being a big company
* fantastic co-workers
* good management chain above me
reviews.cons:
* Questionable whether my work/life balance is right
* pushing hard on the technology to maintain our position in front of the pack
reviews.advice: Not really.  I'm not seeing any serious mistakes.

reviews.title: Was great, they had a lot of intern events
reviews.pros:
- lots of intern events
- manager was nice, helpful, and understanding
- work was interesting
reviews.cons:
- the team lead seemed very stressed and missed a lot of one-on-ones
- I was the only intern on my team, so I didn't interact with too many people and also skipped many online intern events because I didn't want to fall behind on work (this was during covid)

reviews.title: Nvidia is 10 stars out of 5
reviews.pros: Nvidia has a truly visionary leadership team and amazing scientists and engineers. It is the most valuable company for all the good reasons!
reviews.cons: What do you mean cons? No cons! Had my wife allowed me, I would have worked for Nvidia for free!
reviews.advice: Keep up the start-up feel and the benefits of a gigantic company.

reviews.title: Post silicon validation intern
reviews.pros: Great work culture and people.
reviews.cons: None, it's a great company.

reviews.title: NVIDIA Operations Intern
reviews.pros: Great culture and everyone is super welcoming
reviews.cons: Everyone is very busy and can be hard to communicate with manager when needed.

reviews.title: great company
reviews.pros: great company with great values
reviews.cons: there is no cons for this company

reviews.title: Recent retiree after 20+ years
reviews.pros: Genuine sense of shared common goals among teams and employees. Very inclusive culture. Information is freely shared by upper management.
reviews.cons: Overcommunicating is encouraged which generates a lot of noise on multiple email and messaging apps used by the company. But action items are generally easy to spot through alerts.
reviews.advice: Stick with one preferred means of communication for email, etc.

reviews.title: Factory culture
reviews.pros: Fast paced, innovative and very technical
reviews.cons: It's a factory with a whiplash as incentive.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.pros: Great company good work life balance
reviews.cons: No cons all is good

reviews.title: NVIDIA - Where people come to do their lifes work!
reviews.pros:
Best CEO on the planet
Flexible work/life balance
Incredible ESPP and compensation package
Always working on the cutting edge of technology
reviews.cons: Incredible growth is turning it into large corporation-growing bureaucracy
reviews.advice:
Encourage employees to work in various roles within the company
Keep the number of management layers to a minimum
Invest in state of the art technology for sales/marketing teams and not just engineering

reviews.title: Everything is fine
reviews.pros: Peer teams are great to work with.
reviews.cons:
Customer stakeholders, not so much.
Resource planning is not a thing.
Lanes are siloed to the point of obscurity

reviews.title: The best
reviews.pros: Best company to work for
reviews.cons: Benefits could be better compared to competitors

reviews.title: Great employer
reviews.pros: Great product, competent workforce etc.
reviews.cons: Culture can be toxic at times

reviews.title: Best Big Co. to work for
reviews.pros: Lots of freedom to work on what you are interested in
reviews.cons: The typical cons of a big company, but less-so than other places. But they are over-hiring recently, more incompetent people to deal with
reviews.advice: Careful with the over-hiring, the culture is degrading and there may be a crisis in mid 2026 when the big stock vests run out and senior folk start retiring.
